content:
# Groundwater Quality

**Summary**: The study of the chemical and biological parameters that determine if groundwater is safe for human use.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011]] focused on groundwater-quality to assess the impact of local activities on water resources.

- [[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019]] evaluated the groundwater quality in the Wasia aquifer.

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] evaluated the groundwater quality in the study area.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] evaluated the groundwater-quality of both aquifers.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011]] ['heavy metal analysis', 'nutrient assessment', 'risk indexing']

- [[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019]] evaluated the groundwater quality in the Wasia aquifer.

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] evaluated the groundwater quality in the study area.

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] evaluated the groundwater-quality of both aquifers.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011]] ['hydrochemical-analysis', 'environmental-risk-assessment']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Deep groundwater was found to be contaminated with BOD, COD, Nitrate, and Nitrite. ([[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011, p. 127]])

- Only one well showed a good water type for drinking according to WQI. ([[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019, p. 57]])

- Water is suitable for irrigation but requires treatment before drinking. ([[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020, p. 14]])

- Both aquifers are not suitable for domestic drinking water due to high TDS, chloride, and sulfate. ([[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992, p. 117]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2011-alsadeq|Alsadeq, 2011]] Identifying the source of contamination (agricultural vs. industrial).
